segments.speaker: JULIE
segments.en: I am, yes.

segments.speaker: CARL
segments.en: Excellent.
segments.zh: 太好了。那您有什么过敏的吗？

segments.speaker: CARL
segments.en: And do you have any allergies?

segments.speaker: JULIE
segments.en: No, none that I'm aware of.

segments.speaker: CARL
segments.en: OK, well let's have a closer look and see what might be causing this problem.
segments.zh: 好的，那我们仔细看看是什么导致了这个问题。请您站起来一下……

segments.speaker: CARL
segments.en: If you can just get up ...

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: We'll be arriving at Branley Castle in about five minutes, but before we get there I'll give you a little information about the castle and what our visit will include.
segments.zh:
我们大约五分钟后到达布兰利城堡，但在到达之前，我先给大家介绍一下城堡的情况以及我们的参观内容。
实际上，这个地方建城堡已经超过一千一百年了。最早的建筑是一座堡垒，建于公元914年，由当时统治英格兰的阿尔弗雷德大帝的女儿建造，用于防御丹麦入侵者。接下来的一个世纪，诺曼人征服英格兰后，这片土地被赐予一位名叫理查德·德维尔的贵族，他在那里建造了一座城堡，并在德维尔家族中传承了四百多年。
然而，当伊丽莎白一世女王在1576年宣布要访问这座城堡时，城堡已经开始显得破旧不堪，于是决定不修缮客房，而是在主厅旁边用木头为她建造一座新房子。她在那里住了四晚，据说非常豪华，但不幸的是，几年后这座房子被大火烧毁了。
17世纪，城堡属于富有的费尼斯家族，他们扩建了城堡，使其更加舒适。然而，到了1982年，费尼斯家族即使有政府支持，也无力再维护城堡，于是将其挂牌出售。最终，一家拥有多个游乐园的公司接管了城堡，但当我们到达时，我想你们会看到他们成功地保留了城堡原有的氛围。
进入城堡后，你们会发现在国事厅里有栩栩如生的移动蜡像，穿着不同历史时期的服装，甚至还能互相交谈。此外，每个房间里都有小册子，介绍房间的用途以及其中物品和家具的历史。
城堡公园相当广阔。曾经在那里养过羊，19世纪时，主人还建了一个小动物园，里面有兔子甚至小象。如今，旧的动物园建筑被用于公开展示绘画和雕塑。公园里还有一些美丽的树木，但最古老的那棵有800年树龄的树，不幸在1987年被风吹倒了。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So in fact there's been a castle on this site for over eleven hundred years.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The first building was a fort constructed in 914 AD for defence against Danish invaders by King Alfred the Great's daughter, who ruled England at the time.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In the following century, after the Normans conquered England, the land was given to a nobleman called Richard de Vere, and he built a castle there that stayed in the de Vere family for over four hundred years.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: However, when Queen Elizabeth I announced that she was going to visit the castle in 1576 it was beginning to look a bit run down, and it was decided that rather than repair the guest rooms, they'd make a new house for her out of wood next to the main hall.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: She stayed there for four nights and apparently it was very luxurious, but unfortunately it was destroyed a few years later by fire.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In the seventeenth century the castle belonged to the wealthy Fenys family, who enlarged it and made it more comfortable.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: However, by 1982 the Fenys family could no longer afford to maintain the castle, even though they received government support, and they put it on the market.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It was eventually taken over by a company who owned a number of amusement parks, but when we get there I think you'll see that they've managed to retain the original atmosphere of the castle.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: When you go inside, you'll find that in the state rooms there are life-like moving wax models dressed in costumes of different periods in the past, which even carry on conversations together.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: As well as that, in every room there are booklets giving information about what the room was used for and the history of the objects and furniture it contains.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The castle park's quite extensive.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: At one time sheep were kept there, and in the nineteenth century the owners had a little zoo with animals like rabbits and even a baby elephant.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Nowadays the old zoo buildings are used for public displays of paintings and sculpture.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The park also has some beautiful trees, though the oldest of all, which dated back 800 years, was sadly blown down in 1987.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Now, you're free to wander around on your own until 4.30, but then at the end of our visit we'll all meet together at the bottom of the Great Staircase.
segments.zh: 现在，你们可以自由参观到4点30分，但参观结束时，我们将在主楼梯底部集合。然后我们会前往长廊，那里有一组精彩的照片，展示了一百年前城堡的主人在温室里喝茶吃蛋糕的情景——然后我们会带你们去同一个地方，那里将为你们提供下午茶。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: We'll then go on to the long gallery, where there's a wonderful collection of photographs showing the family who owned the castle a hundred years ago having tea and cakes in the conservatory – and we'll then take you to the same place, where afternoon tea will be served to you.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Now if you can take a look at your plans you'll see Branley Castle has four towers, joined together by a high wall, with the river on two sides.
segments.zh:
现在，如果你们看一下地图，会发现布兰利城堡有四座塔楼，由高墙连接，两侧有河流。
不要错过参观大厅。它位于主塔楼靠近河流的地方，是最大的塔楼，在18世纪进行了扩建和重新设计。
如果想欣赏整个城堡的景色，可以沿着城墙走一圈。起点就在主入口附近——沿着小路直走到南门，就在那里。不要去北门——那里没有上去的路。
稍后有一场表演，你们可以看到弓箭手展示他们的箭术。最快的路线是从主入口后第一个左转，沿着小路经过桥，然后就会看到它在尽头。
如果喜欢动物，还有猎鸟展示——猎鹰、鹰等。从主入口向南门方向走，但在到达之前右转而不是穿过南门，经过第一座塔楼后，它就在你的右边。
下午3点，在户外舞台有一场传统舞蹈表演。就在城堡的另一端，过了桥。步行大约十分钟。
最后是商店。它实际上在一座塔楼里面，但入口在外面。从主入口后第一个左转，沿着小路走，第一个右转。那里有一些可爱的礼品和纪念品。
好了，我们就要到了……

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Don't miss seeing the Great Hall.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: That's near the river in the main tower, the biggest one, which was extended and redesigned in the eighteenth century.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: If you want to get a good view of the whole castle, you can walk around the walls.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The starting point's quite near the main entrance – walk straight down the path until you get to the south gate, and it's just there.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Don't go on to the north gate – there's no way up from there.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: There'll shortly be a show in which you can see archers displaying their skill with a bow and arrow.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The quickest way to get there is to take the first left after the main entrance and follow the path past the bridge, then you'll see it in front of you at the end.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: If you like animals there's also a display of hunting birds – falcons and eagles and so on.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: If you go from the main entrance in the direction of the south gate, but turn right before you get there instead of going through it, you'll see it on your right past the first tower.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: At 3 pm there's a short performance of traditional dancing on the outdoor stage.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: That's right at the other side of the castle from the entrance, and over the bridge.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It's about ten minutes' walk or so.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And finally the shop.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It's actually inside one of the towers, but the way in is from the outside.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Just take the first left after the main entrance, go down the path and take the first right.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It's got some lovely gifts and souvenirs.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Right, so we're just arriving ...

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: So, Rosie and Martin, let's look at what you've got for your presentation on woolly mammoths.
segments.zh: 那么，罗茜和马丁，我们来看看你们为猛犸象的展示准备了什么。

segments.speaker: ROSIE
segments.en: OK, we've got a short outline here.
segments.zh: 好的，我们这里有一个简短的提纲。

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: Thanks.
segments.zh: 谢谢。所以是关于北美的一个研究项目？

segments.speaker: TUTOR
segments.en: So it's about a research project in North America?

items.prompt: Before Queen Elizabeth I visited the castle in 1576,

pairs.q: Queen Elizabeth I visited the castle in 1576
pairs.p: Queen Elizabeth I announced that she was going to visit the castle in 1576
pairs.note: 题干和原文几乎一致，只是动词从'visited'变为'announced that she was going to visit'，但时间、人物、地点完全相同。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的'Before Queen Elizabeth I visited the castle in 1576'与原文中的'when Queen Elizabeth I announced that she was going to visit the castle in 1576'对应，时间点一致，因此定位到该句。

items.prompt: In 1982, the castle was sold to

pairs.note: 题干和原文中都是1982年，直接对应。
sold to（卖给）与taken over by（被接管）意思相近，都表示所有权转移。
题干和原文中都是castle，直接对应。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'by 1982 the Fenys family could no longer afford to maintain the castle... and they put it on the market. It was eventually taken over by a company...'，其中'taken over by'对应题干'sold to'，因此答案应为公司。

items.prompt: In some of the rooms, visitors can

pairs.note: 题干中的rooms直接对应原文中的rooms，没有变化。
题干中的visitors can（游客可以）被原文中的you'll find that（你会发现）替换，都是引导游客能做什么。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问游客在房间里可以做什么，定位到原文中'When you go inside, you'll find that in the state rooms there are...'，其中'rooms'和'you'll find that'对应题干。

items.prompt: In the castle park, visitors can

pairs.note: 原文直接出现 'castle park'，与题干完全一致。

pairs.p: public displays of paintings and sculpture
pairs.note: 题干问游客能做什么，原文说旧动物园建筑用于公开展示绘画和雕塑，即游客可以参观这些展览。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问在城堡公园游客可以做什么，定位到原文 'The castle park's quite extensive... Nowadays the old zoo buildings are used for public displays of paintings and sculpture.' 即游客可以参观绘画和雕塑展览。

items.prompt: At the end of the visit, the group will have

pairs.q: at the end of the visit
pairs.p: at the end of our visit
pairs.note: 题干和原文几乎一样，直接定位

pairs.p: afternoon tea will be served to you
pairs.note: 题干说‘小组将会有’，原文说‘下午茶会提供给你们’，意思相同

paraphrase.explain: 题干问参观结束时小组将有什么活动，原文在‘at the end of our visit’后提到‘afternoon tea will be served’，因此答案是下午茶。

items.prompt: Starting point for walking the walls

pairs.note: 原文直接出现 'starting point'，与题干完全一致。
'walk around the walls' 与 'walking the walls' 同义，只是介词不同。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问的是 'walking the walls' 的起点，原文中 'walk around the walls' 后紧接着 'The starting point's quite near the main entrance'，直接对应。

pairs.p: archers displaying their skill with a bow and arrow
pairs.note: 题干是名词短语，原文是动名词短语，但核心词bow and arrow完全一致，archers替换了display的具体内容。

paraphrase.explain: 题干Bow and arrow display对应原文中archers displaying their skill with a bow and arrow，通过archers和bow and arrow定位到该活动。

pairs.note: 题干和原文完全一致，直接定位。
题干和原文完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的 'Hunting birds display' 在原文中逐字出现，直接定位到该句。

pairs.note: 题干和原文完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干关键词 'Traditional dancing' 在原文中逐字出现，通过该词直接定位到对应活动。

pairs.note: 题干和原文完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干是'Shop'，原文最后一段明确提到'And finally the shop.'，直接定位到该句。
